title: Claude Fable 5 is Banned... Do THIS Right Now
summary: A YouTube video arguing that running AI models locally with tools like Ollama protects against sudden API access loss, demonstrated when Claude Fable 5 was pulled without warning. The creator shows how to build a free NotebookLM alternative and set up multi-model routing using OpenRouter, Claude, and DeepSeek.
writeup: The video uses the abrupt removal of Claude Fable 5 to argue that depending on a single API is risky, and that local inference with Ollama preserves access when a provider pulls a model without warning. It walks through building a free alternative to NotebookLM and configuring multi-model routing across OpenRouter, Claude, and DeepSeek. The takeaways center on local model hosting as a hedge against sudden API loss and routing setups that spread workloads across several models rather than one vendor.
